source.multipart_basis: The original sample specifies body_count=assembly with 'pedestal with rebar cage as sub-bodies'. Multipart decomposition formalizes the concrete pedestal block, longitudinal rebar, transverse stirrup/tie rebar, crusher anchor bolts, and the Winkler soil-spring boundary as distinct part families.

full_prompt:
Design the SME CN9 Aggregate Competition jaw crusher foundation pedestal as a multi-part assembly, explicitly modeling the 4.5 m x 3.0 m x 1.8 m reinforced concrete block as the physical collection of the concrete pedestal body, ASTM A615 Grade 60 longitudinal rebar mat, transverse stirrup/tie rebar, crusher anchor bolt assemblies, and the machine base-frame pad that interfaces the crusher to the pedestal top. The original prompt already calls out 'deliver STEP AP242 solid body of the pedestal with rebar cage as sub-bodies'; this multipart sidecar formalizes that decomposition.

The assembly consists of five part families: (A) the concrete pedestal body, a rectangular 4.5 m long x 3.0 m wide x 1.8 m deep block of normal-weight reinforced concrete fc' 27.6 MPa; (B) longitudinal rebar mats in the top and bottom faces of the pedestal (and side faces for shrinkage/temperature), ASTM A615 Grade 60 with team-selected bar size and spacing to satisfy ACI 318-19 Chapter 22 moment/shear DCR; (C) transverse stirrup/tie rebar that confines the longitudinal mat and provides shear reinforcement for the overhung surcharge + machine loads; (D) anchor-bolt assemblies at the crusher footprint pattern (typical 8-16 anchor bolts on the jaw-crusher base pad) that transmit the 735 kN dead + 90 kN vertical dynamic + 30 kN horizontal dynamic forces from the crusher base into the pedestal; (E) the crusher base-frame pad (steel plate or cast leveling pad) that distributes the machine base footprint load into the pedestal top.

Geometry inherits the original sample verbatim: pedestal 4.5 m x 3.0 m x 1.8 m, feed opening 1100 x 800 mm (jaw-crusher footprint centered on pedestal).

Material inherits verbatim: concrete fc' 27.6 MPa; rebar ASTM A615 Grade 60 (Fy 414 MPa); soil allowable bearing 250 kPa with subgrade modulus 40 MPa. Anchor bolts are typically ASTM F1554 Grade 55 (added by the multipart sidecar for concreteness; not specified by the original).

Loads inherit verbatim: LC1 crusher dead 735 kN centered; LC2 vertical dynamic 90 kN amplitude at 4.17 Hz; LC3 horizontal dynamic 30 kN amplitude at 4.17 Hz; LC4 hopper surcharge 1570 kN at one end.

Pass/fail criteria inherit verbatim from the original and apply at the assembly level: (R1) peak soil bearing pressure under 1.2D + 1.6L + 1.0E combination <= 250 kPa; (R2) max rebar DCR per ACI 318-19 Chapter 22 <= 1.0 across all load cases; (R3) pedestal-top vertical velocity amplitude at 4.17 Hz <= 5.0 mm/s rms per DIN 4150-3; (R4) first natural frequency of the pedestal-soil system >= 1.5x 4.17 Hz (>= 6.255 Hz) or <= 0.5x 4.17 Hz (<= 2.085 Hz). Added assembly-level targets under fem_check_policy.assembled_only: anchor-bolt pullout/shear DCR and rebar-to-concrete bond development DCR must be reported; per-part stresses are reported for diagnostic purposes but do not gate pass/fail.

Thermal, fluid, and radiation analyses are not required; buckling is not required. Forced harmonic analysis (vibration) is required for LC2/LC3 harmonic machine forces. A machine-foundation lumped-parameter stiffness calculator (DIN 4024 / Richart) is required outside the FEA for the natural-frequency check. Deliver STEP AP242 of the assembly with each body (concrete pedestal, each rebar mat, each stirrup run, each anchor bolt, base-frame pad) as a distinct solid tagged with part_family (A..E) and instance_index, plus a structural report listing peak bearing pressure, rebar DCR, pedestal-top vertical velocity amplitude, first natural frequency, assembly mass, and anchor-bolt / rebar-bond DCR.
short_prompt:
Design the SME CN9 jaw-crusher RC foundation pedestal as a multi-part assembly (concrete pedestal body, longitudinal rebar mats, transverse stirrup/tie rebar, anchor bolts, machine base-frame pad).

Pedestal 4.5 m x 3.0 m x 1.8 m; concrete fc' 27.6 MPa; rebar ASTM A615 Grade 60 (Fy 414 MPa); soil allowable bearing 250 kPa, subgrade modulus 40 MPa; anchor bolts ASTM F1554 Grade 55; feed opening 1100 x 800 mm.

Load cases: LC1 crusher dead 735 kN centred; LC2 vertical dynamic 90 kN amplitude at 4.17 Hz; LC3 horizontal dynamic 30 kN amplitude at 4.17 Hz; LC4 hopper surcharge 1570 kN at one end.

Pass/fail: peak soil bearing <= 250 kPa; rebar DCR <= 1.0 per ACI 318-19 Chapter 22; pedestal-top vertical velocity <= 5.0 mm/s rms per DIN 4150-3; first natural frequency >= 6.255 Hz OR <= 2.085 Hz (DIN 4024 around 4.17 Hz); FEM at assembled level; anchor + rebar-bond DCR <= 1.0.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: Soil-foundation lumped-parameter stiffness and damping per DIN 4024/Richart is a closed-form block-on-halfspace model used alongside FEA for the natural-frequency check.

prompt.title: Jaw crusher RC foundation pedestal 4.5 x 3.0 x 1.8 m (multi-part assembly)
prompt.brief: Multi-part RC pedestal for 75 t jaw crusher: concrete block (A) + longitudinal rebar mat (B) + stirrups/ties (C) + anchor bolts (D) + machine base pad (E). 4.5 x 3.0 x 1.8 m. 90 kN vertical + 30 kN horizontal dynamic at 4.17 Hz plus 160 t hopper surcharge. Verified at assembly level: soil bearing <= 250 kPa, ACI 318-19 rebar DCR <= 1.0, DIN 4150-3 vertical velocity <= 5.0 mm/s, natural freq >= 6.255 Hz or <= 2.085 Hz, anchor/bond DCR <= 1.0.

parts.name: concrete_pedestal_body
parts.role: 4.5 m long x 3.0 m wide x 1.8 m deep reinforced concrete block; carries crusher dead + dynamic + surcharge loads and distributes them into the soil via the 4.5 x 3.0 m footprint

parts.name: longitudinal_rebar_mat
parts.role: ASTM A615 Grade 60 longitudinal rebar mat in the top and bottom faces of the pedestal (and optional side faces); provides flexural reinforcement against the hopper surcharge overturning moment
parts.count_note: 2 mats minimum (top + bottom); side mats optional per team design

parts.name: transverse_stirrup_tie
parts.role: transverse stirrups/ties confining the longitudinal rebar mat and providing shear reinforcement; spaced per ACI 318-19 Chapter 22 shear design

parts.name: crusher_anchor_bolt
parts.role: anchor bolt assembly at the jaw-crusher base pad; transmits 735 kN dead + 90 kN vertical dynamic + 30 kN horizontal dynamic forces into the pedestal through pullout, shear, and bending reactions
parts.count_note: typical jaw-crusher base pad uses 8-16 anchor bolts per manufacturer spec

parts.name: crusher_base_frame_pad
parts.role: steel or grouted leveling pad between the crusher base frame and the pedestal top; distributes the machine base-frame footprint load into the pedestal
parts.material_hint: ASTM A572 Gr 50 plate or non-shrink grout

assembly_envelope.coordinate_system: pedestal coordinates: +x along 4.5 m length, +y along 3.0 m width, +z up from bottom

boundary_conditions.soil: lumped vertical + horizontal + rocking springs per DIN 4024 or Winkler base

objective.primary: pass bearing, ACI DCR, vibration, and resonance checks under assembled_only FEM check

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: DIN 4024 resonance separation around 4.17 Hz forcing
ACI 318-19 Chapter 17 anchor-to-concrete + Chapter 25 rebar development DCR

recommended_fea_setup.element_type: solid concrete with embedded reinforcement, Winkler soil

interfaces.load_path: rebar-to-concrete bond along the longitudinal mat; develops flexural tension reinforcement for the surcharge overturning moment
stirrup tied to longitudinal bars; confines concrete and carries shear reinforcement
cast-in anchor bolt embedded in the pedestal top; transmits crusher dead + dynamic + horizontal loads via pullout and shear
grouted leveling pad / steel base frame interface between the crusher base and the pedestal top
anchor bolt through the machine base-frame pad clamps the crusher foot to the pedestal

fem_check_policy.rationale: Multipart entries are verified on the assembled model. Per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail.

notes.why_multi_part: RC machine foundations are always cast concrete + rebar + anchor-bolt assemblies; the multipart sidecar formalizes that into a concrete block, rebar mats, stirrups, and anchor bolts.
notes.speculation_caveats: Exact rebar size/spacing and anchor-bolt count are team-selected to satisfy ACI 318-19 DCR and manufacturer anchor pattern.
